attributes.description: Alerts when the broker JVM heap utilization exceeds 85% (`heap.used / heap.max > 0.85`). Sustained high heap triggers GC thrashing and often precedes OutOfMemoryError.
attributes.name: [ActiveMQ OTel] High JVM heap utilization

investigation_guide.blob:
## ActiveMQ JVM heap utilization high

### What fired
`activemq.jvm.memory.heap.used / activemq.jvm.memory.heap.max` peaked above 0.85 over the evaluation window.

### Why it matters
When the JVM cannot reclaim enough heap, GC pauses lengthen and become more frequent. Message processing stalls during major collections, destination memory fills up, and a full heap eventually crashes the broker with `OutOfMemoryError`.

### Triage
1. Broker Health dashboard: confirm heap trend vs `.committed` and `.max`.
2. Correlate with broker memory utilization (`activemq.memory.utilization`) — these often move together.
3. Look at GC metrics for a sustained upward step in `activemq.jvm.gc.collections` or `.duration` rates.
4. Watch `activemq.jvm.thread.count` — thread leaks can drive heap growth.

### Remediation
- Raise `-Xmx` if the workload has legitimately grown.
- Investigate leaks: unbounded DLQ, long-lived subscriptions, custom plugins holding references.
- Ensure `storeCursor` / `fileCursor` is used for large queues instead of `vmCursor` which keeps everything in memory.

### Tuning
- Threshold `> 0.85` matches the documented "warning" band. Tighten to 0.80 for tight clusters.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-activemq.otel-*
// JVM heap metrics are broker-level (no destination dimension)
| WHERE activemq.jvm.memory.heap.used IS NOT NULL AND activemq.jvm.memory.heap.max IS NOT NULL AND activemq.jvm.memory.heap.max > 0
| STATS heap_used = MAX(LAST_OVER_TIME(activemq.jvm.memory.heap.used)), heap_max = MAX(LAST_OVER_TIME(activemq.jvm.memory.heap.max)) BY activemq.broker.name, host.name
| EVAL heap_util = heap_used / heap_max
// Alert when heap utilization exceeds 85%; adjust threshold for your environment
| WHERE heap_util > 0.85
| SORT heap_util DESC
| LIMIT 10
